executive_summary.narrative: This release combines project-level IAM over-privilege with confirmed live privilege-escalation and root execution on running pods. The combination represents a direct path from container compromise to broad GCP access.

cross_domain_correlations.title: Excessive IAM Privilege Compounds Live Privilege Escalation
cross_domain_correlations.description: A project-level Owner role is granted to a service account that backs workloads where privilege escalation and root execution are both confirmed enabled live.
cross_domain_correlations.business_impact: A compromised container can escalate to full project access with no additional barrier.
cross_domain_correlations.recommended_action: Remove the Owner role and harden pod securityContexts together — fixing either alone leaves the other half of the path intact.

top_risks.title: Live privilege escalation and root execution
top_risks.impact: Confirmed by Kyverno on running pods, not a hypothetical misconfiguration.
top_risks.why_it_matters: This is the most direct path to container breakout.
top_risks.recommended_action: Set allowPrivilegeEscalation: false and runAsNonRoot: true across all workloads.

priority_actions.title: Harden pod securityContexts
priority_actions.rationale: Resolves both live Kyverno violations in one Helm values change.
priority_actions.expected_risk_reduction: Eliminates the confirmed privilege-escalation and root-execution path.

release_readiness.rationale: Multiple critical, live, high-confidence findings with a confirmed compounding path between infrastructure IAM and runtime privilege escalation.

assumptions_and_unknowns.impact_on_assessment: Application-layer scanning was skipped; code-level risk is entirely unassessed for this release.
